title: Gmail thread to task (egress-gated bonus)
description: Add `bober task from-gmail <thread>` which, only when an opt-in gmail egress axis is enabled, reads a Gmail thread through the MCP connector, parses it locally into an action-Finding (title from the thread subject), and captures it via captureTask. With the axis off (default) the command refuses with a clear opt-in message and makes no network call.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` exits 0 with the from-gmail subcommand and the opt-in gmail egress gate added.
A unit test runs from-gmail with the gmail axis disabled (default) and asserts the command returns an opt-in refusal, sets exitCode=1, and never constructs/starts an MCP client (no network).
A unit test runs from-gmail with the axis enabled and a stubbed MCP client returning a thread payload, asserting one open action-Finding is captured with title derived from the thread subject.
A unit test simulates an MCP/connector error and asserts the handler catches it, sets exitCode=1, and the error message contains no connector env/token contents.
`npm run typecheck` exits 0 — the Gmail-thread parser returns a typed Finding accepted by captureTask.

nonGoals: Do NOT enable Gmail egress by default — the axis must default false and gate every network call.
Do NOT build a general Gmail integration/sync — read one thread on demand and parse it locally.
Do NOT include connector env vars or tokens in any error/log message (mirror src/mcp/external-client.ts:66-77).
Do NOT bypass captureTask — the parsed thread is captured through the sprint-1 write path.
Do NOT add new runtime dependencies beyond the already-present MCP SDK.
stopConditions: `npm run build` and `npm run typecheck` exit 0 and the gated/enabled/error from-gmail unit tests pass.
from-gmail refuses with no network when the axis is off and captures a task when the axis is on with a stubbed connector.
definitionOfDone: A user who has explicitly enabled the gmail egress axis can run `bober task from-gmail <thread>` to pull a Gmail thread through the MCP connector, have it parsed locally into an open action-Finding (title from the subject), and captured into the hub pool. With the axis off (the default), the command refuses with an opt-in message and performs zero Gmail egress. Connector failures are caught, reported on stderr with exitCode=1, and never leak env/token contents.
assumptions: A gmail egress axis follows the EgressGuard opt-in pattern (default false, assertAllowed throws if not enabled) — src/medical/egress.ts:1-59.

The Gmail MCP connector is invoked via the ExternalMcpServer.callTool surface (src/mcp/external-client.ts:95-103); the tool name and arg shape are read from listTools at runtime, and the connector is injectable so tests can stub it.
Thread parsing is a pure local function: payload -> {title:<subject>, kind:'action', status:'open'}; it does not call the network itself.
outOfScope: Telegram document upload / mobile capture (telegram scope).
Calendar event creation from threads (calendar scope).
Ongoing Gmail polling or label automation.
generatorNotes: Create src/hub/gmail-to-task.ts with: (1) a pure parseGmailThread(payload) -> Finding-shaped object (title from subject, kind='action', status='open', tags maybe ['source:gmail']); (2) fromGmailTask({egressAllowed, mcp, threadRef, store, now}) that returns/throws an opt-in error when egressAllowed is false BEFORE touching mcp, else mcp.callTool(<gmail-read-tool>, {thread:threadRef}), parse, and captureTask. Add a `from-gmail <thread>` subcommand to src/cli/commands/task.ts that resolves the gmail axis from config (default false), and on disabled prints a chalk.yellow opt-in message + exitCode=1 + return (no MCP construction). Reuse ExternalMcpServer (src/mcp/external-client.ts); inject it so tests stub callTool. Wrap MCP calls in try/catch; sanitize errors (strip KEY=VALUE patterns as external-client.ts:69 does) and never print env/tokens. Keep now injected.
evaluatorNotes: Verify the default (axis off) path makes NO MCP construction or network call — the test must assert the stub's callTool was never invoked. Verify enabling the axis + a stubbed thread payload captures one open action-Finding via captureTask (reused). Verify error sanitization: feed an error string containing a fake TOKEN=secret and assert it does not appear in the surfaced message. Confirm parseGmailThread is pure (no network/clock) and captureTask is the only write path.
